caption: Modality confusion measures how often adding a second input modality makes a model forget a label it got right unimodally (lower is better). When μ(A) > μ(V), the model forgets audible classes more often than visible ones — a sign it prefers the video modality. When μ(A) ≈ μ(V), the model treats both modalities in a balanced way.
_note: muA values for Gemini 2.0 Flash (2.07) and Unified-IO 2 (10.20) were recovered from corrupted cells in static/tables/main-results.html — confirm against the paper before publishing.
